text.title: strict-mode-required-fields Core Rules
text.summary: Five testable rules for producing a Pydantic / JSON-Schema model that passes the OpenAI / Azure / Anthropic strict-mode compiler.

rule.statement: For every object schema submitted under `strict: true`, the `required` array must equal the set of keys in `properties`. No property declared on an object may be absent from `required`.
rule.rationale: Strict-mode compiles the schema into a regular grammar; an "optional" key creates branch non-determinism (skip token vs read token). The OpenAI error `400 Invalid schema for response_format ... required list does not match properties` is the compiler refusing that branch.

rule.statement: Every object schema must set `additionalProperties: false`. Missing or `additionalProperties: true` is a strict-mode rejection.
rule.rationale: Without this, the grammar must accept arbitrary key/value tokens at every object boundary, which collapses the determinism guarantee.

rule.statement: Stub rule for conclusion 'fm-01-empty-string-sentinel' referenced from 06-decision-tree.xml. Replace with the real testable rule that resolves this branch of the tree.
rule.rationale: Auto-added by fix-methodology-phase-d.py to keep tree refs resolvable.

rule.statement: Stub rule for conclusion 'fm-02-nullable-money' referenced from 06-decision-tree.xml. Replace with the real testable rule that resolves this branch of the tree.
rule.rationale: Auto-added by fix-methodology-phase-d.py to keep tree refs resolvable.

rule.statement: Stub rule for conclusion 'fm-05-metadata-bag-in-strict' referenced from 06-decision-tree.xml. Replace with the real testable rule that resolves this branch of the tree.
rule.rationale: Auto-added by fix-methodology-phase-d.py to keep tree refs resolvable.

rule.statement: Encode "may be absent" by declaring the field as `T | None = None` and keeping it in `required`. Never drop a property from `required` to mean "optional" under strict mode. Empty-string / sentinel encodings are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Strict-mode bridges translate `T | None` into `{"type": ["T", "null"]}` and keep the property required, which is the only encoding the FSM accepts. Sentinels (`""`, `-1`) hide absence and degrade data quality silently.
rule.reference: Reference shape with `notes: str | None = None` encoded as nullable-required.

rule.statement: Set `model_config = ConfigDict(extra="forbid")` on every Pydantic model whose schema is consumed under strict mode. Apply it to nested models as well — strict mode walks the whole tree.
rule.rationale: `extra="forbid"` is the Pydantic flag that emits `additionalProperties: false` in the JSON Schema. Without it the model defaults to permissive and the schema fails strict-mode submission.

rule.statement: A CI test must call `assert_strict_schema(Model)` (or the equivalent JSON-walker) for every model used in a strict-mode call. The walker asserts on every object node: (a) `additionalProperties is False`, (b) `set(properties.keys()) == set(required)`. CI failure here blocks the merge.
rule.rationale: The strict-mode 400 fires at request time, not build time. Without a CI assertion, the regression escapes review and production rejects the first traffic that uses the new model.
rule.reference: Includes the canonical `assert_strict_schema()` walker; copy verbatim into CI tests.
